title: Manual segmentation of histology images
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8g7nr","text":"Here we manually segmented histology images to denote functional tissue units, using the QuPath application.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: View histology image (TIFF file) in QuPath software program .
Pathologist circled the identified functional tissue region(s).

The masked regions were exported as TIFF files. We used the following script to export the mask from QuPath to coordinates in geoJSON format. This script is specific to segmented tissue regions in the Fallopian tube but should be easily adapted to other tissue and regions. https://gist.github.com/kuang-da/f42dbe92caa9ef24924ea0b626a4535c

Once we had the mask coordinates in the geoJSON file, we used this script to convert the coordinates from the geoJSON file into a binary mask in aTIFF file. This script is specific to segmented tissue regions in the Fallopian tube but should be easily adapted to other tissue and regions. https://gist.github.com/kuang-da/aac075af6e121dae4e45f9ee82e087ef
creator: Stephen Fisher
